repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem:
Deleting another buffer from a float could move focus into the holder window and fire BufEnter for the buffer being deleted.

Solution:
Use switch_win_noblock() instead of buf_jump_open_win() before recursing into do_buffer_ext().

Fix #39827

comment_data.body:
still must call if `switch_win_noblock` returned fail, but it looks infallible here (maybe assert instead?)
also could replace the `win_find_tabpage` call by saving its `tp` from the loop?

the comment seems confusing given that `curwin->w_floating` (not a "normal window"?) is checked just below it?

is the old `close_windows keeps the last non-float window.` comment worth keeping in some form?

should this (or elsewhere in this area) also mention that it's avoided BufEnter events, and any other hints about the goal of this code? this is a lot of code and the purpose will just be forgotten in time...
the old `close_windows` call is still here, above the new one. is that intentional?

comment_regular_data.body: Looks fine. If changing the semantics of close_windows is okay

repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

title: fix(float): `:bufdelete` in floatwin may change focus
